title: Animal Control Workers
description: Handle animals for the purpose of investigations of mistreatment, or control of abandoned, dangerous, or unattended animals.

tasks.text: Write reports of activities, and maintain files of impoundments and dispositions of animals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Investigate reports of animal attacks or animal cruelty, interviewing witnesses, collecting evidence, and writing reports.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine animals for injuries or malnutrition, and arrange for any necessary medical treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Contact animal owners to inform them that their pets are at animal holding facilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Educate the public about animal welfare, and animal control laws and regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove captured animals from animal-control service vehicles and place animals in shelter cages or other enclosures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer inquiries from the public concerning animal control operations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Capture and remove stray, uncontrolled, or abused animals from undesirable conditions, using nets, nooses, or tranquilizer darts as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare for prosecutions related to animal treatment, and give evidence in court.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supply animals with food, water, and personal care.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Issue warnings or citations in connection with animal-related offenses, or contact police to report violations and request arrests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Euthanize rabid, unclaimed, or severely injured animals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean facilities and equipment such as dog pens and animal control trucks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Organize the adoption of unclaimed animals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine animal licenses, and inspect establishments housing animals for compliance with laws.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Train police officers in dog handling and training techniques for tracking, crowd control, and narcotics and bomb detection.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
